title: Section 1 — Booking a Hotel Room
transcript: Agent: Good morning, Riverside Hotel, how can I help you?
Caller: Hi, I'd like to book a room for two nights, from the 14th to the 16th of March.
Agent: Of course. And how many guests will be staying?
Caller: Just two adults — me and my partner.
Agent: We have a standard double room available for £95 per night, or a superior room with a garden view for £120.
Caller: I'll go for the superior room, please.
Agent: Great choice. Can I take your name?
Agent: Thank you, Ms Huang. And a contact number?
Agent: Perfect. Do you have any dietary requirements for breakfast?
Agent: Noted. Breakfast is served from 7 to 10. Check-in is after 3 pm. Your booking reference is RV4821.

questions.text: The caller wants to book a room from the 14th to the ________ of March.
questions.explanation: The caller says 'from the 14th to the 16th of March'.

questions.text: Which type of room does the caller choose?

options.label: B
options.text: Superior room with a garden view at £120

questions.explanation: The caller says 'I'll go for the superior room' which is £120 per night with a garden view.
The caller spells it out: H-U-A-N-G.

questions.text: What dietary requirement does the caller have?

questions.explanation: The agent confirms the reference as RV4821.
